description:
You are a data warehouse engineer. Build a sales analytics star schema in a new DW schema from AdventureWorks2022.

DW.DimDate: One row per day, 2010-01-01 to 2014-12-31. Columns: DateKey (INT, YYYYMMDD format), FullDate, Year, Quarter, Month, MonthName, DayOfWeek, DayName, IsWeekend (BIT), FiscalYear (July-June: July 2013 = FY2014), FiscalQuarter (Q1=Jul-Sep, Q2=Oct-Dec, Q3=Jan-Mar, Q4=Apr-Jun).

DW.DimProduct: Surrogate key ProductSK (IDENTITY). All products LEFT JOINed to ProductSubcategory and ProductCategory. NULL category = 'Uncategorized', NULL subcategory = 'General'. Include ProductID, ProductName, ProductNumber, CategoryName, SubcategoryName, Color, ListPrice, StandardCost, IsActive (1 if SellEndDate IS NULL).

DW.DimCustomer: Surrogate key CustomerSK (IDENTITY). CustomerName: 'FirstName LastName' for individuals (those with PersonID), 'Store: StoreName' for store-only customers. Include CustomerID, AccountNumber, CustomerType ('Individual'/'Store'), TerritoryName, TerritoryGroup, CountryRegionCode.

DW.FactSales: One row per SalesOrderDetail line. Foreign keys: OrderDateKey, ShipDateKey (NULL if ShipDate NULL) to DimDate.DateKey; ProductSK to DimProduct; CustomerSK to DimCustomer. Include SalesOrderID, SalesOrderDetailID, OrderQty, UnitPrice, UnitPriceDiscount, LineTotal, AllocatedTax (= LineTotal/SubTotal * TaxAmt per order; 0 when SubTotal = 0), AllocatedFreight (same logic), TotalLineAmount (LineTotal + AllocatedTax + AllocatedFreight).

Create stored procedure DW.usp_LoadStarSchema to populate everything with surrogate key lookups. Execute it.

Validation: SUM(FactSales.LineTotal) must equal SUM(Sales.SalesOrderDetail.LineTotal) within $0.01.

Export total revenue (SUM of TotalLineAmount) by FiscalYear and TerritoryGroup to /home/ga/Documents/exports/dw_revenue_summary.csv.

metadata.application: Azure Data Studio + Microsoft SQL Server 2022
metadata.dataset: AdventureWorks2022 Sales (31K orders, 121K line items, 504 products, 19K customers)
metadata.difficulty_rationale: Requires building a complete dimensional model with interdependent tables. The fact table cannot load without correct dimensions. Proportional tax/freight allocation requires careful handling of division by zero and rounding. Fiscal year logic (July-June) is a common off-by-one trap. Customer dimension requires understanding the dual-type relationship (PersonID vs StoreID) in AdventureWorks. Surrogate key lookups across three dimensions create cascading failure modes where a single missing dimension row breaks reconciliation.
